description: Multi-component interaction: Delayed NoExecute taint due to stale observedReadyCondition in processTaintBaseEviction allows scheduler to place pods on unreachable nodes

required_findings.description: Identifies processTaintBaseEviction as the function responsible for adding NoExecute taints based on node ready condition
Identifies that processTaintBaseEviction receives observedReadyCondition (stale) instead of currentReadyCondition (fresh)
Explains that observedReadyCondition is the PREVIOUS cycle's ready state while currentReadyCondition is the just-computed state
Identifies tryUpdateNodeHealth as the function that returns both observedReadyCondition and currentReadyCondition
Explains the timing: first cycle detects Unknown and sets observedReadyCondition=True (stale), second cycle has observedReadyCondition=Unknown and adds NoExecute
Identifies the NoExecuteTaintManager or taint eviction controller as responsible for evicting pods after NoExecute taint is applied
Identifies that the scheduler's TaintToleration plugin filters nodes based on taints during scheduling
Explains that pods with NoSchedule tolerations but without NoExecute tolerations can be scheduled during the delay window
Identifies the kubelet heartbeat mechanism (node status + Lease) as the trigger for the node lifecycle controller
Identifies monitorNodeHealth as the main monitoring loop in the node lifecycle controller
Mentions markNodeForTainting or the taint queue mechanism used to add NoExecute taints
Identifies SwapNodeControllerTaint or the mechanism for swapping between NotReady and Unreachable taints

file_references.description: Identifies pkg/controller/tainteviction/taint_eviction.go (where NoExecuteTaintManager / taint eviction controller lives)
Identifies pkg/controller/util/node/ or controllerutil (node condition utilities used for taint swapping)
Identifies pkg/util/taints/ or taintutils (taint existence checking)
Identifies pkg/kubelet/ or kubelet heartbeat/lease mechanism

causal_chain.description: Component 1 (Kubelet): Node stops sending heartbeats (node status and Lease renewals). After nodeMonitorGracePeriod, the node lifecycle controller detects the node as unresponsive.

Component 2 (Node Lifecycle Controller): tryUpdateNodeHealth sets currentReadyCondition to Unknown and returns observedReadyCondition as the previous Ready=True state. monitorNodeHealth passes the stale observedReadyCondition to processTaintBaseEviction.

Bug: processTaintBaseEviction receives observedReadyCondition=True (stale), sees ConditionTrue, and removes taints/marks node as reachable instead of adding NoExecute taint. The NoExecute taint is delayed until the NEXT monitoring cycle when observedReadyCondition finally reflects Unknown.

Component 3 (Scheduler + Taint Eviction): During the delay window, the scheduler's TaintToleration plugin allows pods with NoSchedule tolerations to be placed on the node. Once NoExecute taint arrives, the taint eviction controller evicts them — but they were already scheduled to an unreachable node.

negative_checks.description: Does NOT blame the kubelet implementation or the heartbeat protocol itself as the root cause
Does NOT claim the bug is in the scheduler's taint filtering logic or in the taint eviction controller itself
